rationale: Iteration 003 wins 3 evals by majority vote (2 unanimously), loses 0, ties 11. No regressions on any eval. The 3 wins are substantial visual improvements: (1) eval-001 ground-level now shows OSM buildings instead of blank sky, (2) eval-005 NYC skyline shows dramatic 3D cityscape instead of flat grid, (3) eval-011 chained flyTo successfully completes both stops. The 11 ties confirm no regressions on existing well-performing evals.

hypotheses_validated.H1_heading_convention_table: TIE on directional evals (003,004,008,013,014) — heading was already correct, table is defensive improvement
hypotheses_validated.H2_gimbal_lock_warning: TIE on top-down evals (002,007) — safe pitch value produces identical visual output
hypotheses_validated.H3_osm_buildings_guidance: WIN on eval-001, eval-005 — dramatic visual improvement from 3D building guidance
hypotheses_validated.H4_zoom_collision_gotcha: TIE on eval-009 — constraint not visually verifiable, defensive improvement
hypotheses_validated.H5_flyhome_limitation: TIE on eval-012 — flyHome behavior unchanged, documentation improvement

key_insight: H3 (OSM Buildings guidance) was the highest-impact hypothesis. Adding a single callout about loading 3D buildings for city views caused the LLM to produce fundamentally different code (loading createOsmBuildingsAsync) which transformed flat imagery into recognizable 3D cityscapes.
